channel.title: Fee estimation on ₿itcoin Transcripts
channel.description: Recent content in Fee estimation on ₿itcoin Transcripts

item.title: Post Cluster Mempool, Mining, and Fee Estimation
item.description: Asynchronous mempool updates via validation interface Return mempool updates before and after the fee rate diagram. (of update affected chunk) To be used for efficient mining and chunk best block policy fee estimation The changes needed are minimal Wire the chunk refs in GetMainStagingDiagrams Refactor GetPackageHash to a generic GetHashFromWitnessIds Cache the chunks returned from GetMainStagingDiagrams Extract the dependency addition of UpdateTransactionsFromBlock into it’s function On each mempool mutation collect the witness id of all chunks removed/added reason for removal chunk fee rate and fire mempool updated validation interface notification

item.title: Cluster Mempool Explained & How Bitcoin Fees Actually Work w/Bitcoin Core Dev
item.description: Pieter Wuille and Shinobi discuss cluster mempool, now merged into Bitcoin Core for the 31.0 release. The conversation covers why the original mempool had conflicting orderings for mining vs eviction, how cluster mempool solves this by partitioning related transactions into clusters of at most 64 transactions and running an optimal ordering algorithm on those small groups, and how this enables a globally consistent pre-computed total ordering that improves replace-by-fee incentive compatibility, fee estimation, multi-child CPFP, and transaction relay rate limiting. The talk also covers the historical progression of mempool improvements and how optimal ordering within bounded clusters future-proofs the mempool for arbitrary layer 2 use cases like Lightning and Ark.
